question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parse(List)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch parse path where count += streamFile.getCount() accumulates across files, and if a file containing a TOKENUNFREEZE has count=0 (because all its items were filtered by dateRangeFilter), the file is treated as empty and its state changes are not committed, leaving the freeze in place?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a record file where all items have consensusTimestamps that fall outside the dateRangeFilter window, causing count to remain 0 and recordFile.setCount(0), but aggregator.update(recordFile) still sets loadEnd and logsBloom, making the file appear successfully processed while no token state changes (including TOKENUNFREEZE) were actually applied?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between @Transactional and @Retryable where a transient database error during TOKENUNFREEZE processing causes a retry, but the retry re-reads the dateRangeFilter from the cache which now has a different start boundary (because the filter was updated between retries), causing the TOKENUNFREEZE to be filtered out on the retry attempt, resulting in a permanent freeze?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where a TOKENFREEZE and TOKENUNFREEZE for the same account appear in the same file, but the TOKENFREEZE has a consensusTimestamp within the dateRangeFilter and the TOKENUNFREEZE has a consensusTimestamp outside it (e.g., at the boundary), causing only the freeze to be persisted via recordItemListener.onItem() while the unfreeze is silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the EntityRecordItemListener has a to-do comment '// to:do - exclude Freeze from Filter transaction type', indicating that TOKENFREEZE transactions may be incorrectly subject to commonParserProperties.getFilter() filtering, causing a TOKENUNFREEZE to be filtered out by the entity filter while the TOKENFREEZE was not, resulting in a permanent freeze?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large number of contract transactions with maximum gas values to cause gasUsed to overflow in RecordItemAggregator, producing a negative gasUsed value in the RecordFile, which causes downstream validation to reject the file and trigger a re-parse that re-applies TOKENFREEZE transactions without the corresponding TOKENUNFREEZE?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Transactional timeout is configured via a SpEL expression #{@recordParserProperties.getTransactionTimeout().toSeconds()}, and if this timeout is very short, a large record file containing a TOKENUNFREEZE near the end will time out before the unfreeze is processed, rolling back the entire transaction while a prior committed file's TOKENFREEZE remains, causing a permanent freeze?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that recordFile.getSidecars() are added to parserContext after all items are processed, and if a sidecar contains data that conflicts with a TOKENUNFREEZE item's state, the sidecar data could overwrite the unfreeze state in the parserContext before it is flushed to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parse(List)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch parse path where if initial == previous (no files were parsed), the method returns early without calling doFlush(), so that if all files in the batch were skipped (e.g., due to shouldParse() returning false for files containing TOKENUNFREEZE), the unfreeze is never committed while the freeze from a prior batch remains?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a record file where the TOKENUNFREEZE transaction is a non-top-level (child) transaction, causing RecordItemAggregator.accept() to return early via !recordItem.isTopLevel(), and if the recordItemListener also has special handling for non-top-level items that skips freeze state updates, the unfreeze is never applied?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: recordMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an unknown transaction type (mapped to unknownLatencyMetric/unknownSizeMetric) that is actually a TOKENUNFREEZE disguised with an unrecognized type code, causing it to be recorded in metrics but processed incorrectly by recordItemListener.onItem(), resulting in the freeze state not being updated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that dateRangeCalculator.getFilter() is called once at the start of doParse() and the result is used for all items in the file, so that if the filter's end boundary (Long.MAX_VALUE when endDate is null) is somehow manipulated to a finite value between the TOKENFREEZE and TOKENUNFREEZE timestamps, the unfreeze is dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer to process a record file containing a TOKENUNFREEZE while the @Transactional annotation's default isolation level allows a concurrent transaction to read the frozen state before the unfreeze is committed, and if that concurrent transaction caches the frozen state, the account appears permanently frozen to that consumer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file transition from v5 to v6 with a zero index value, causing offset = recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1 to be negative, and repository.updateIndex(negative_offset) to decrement all block indices, making previously processed blocks (including those with TOKENUNFREEZE) appear to have lower indices that conflict with existing entries, causing constraint violations that prevent the unfreeze from being re-processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the logIndex AtomicInteger is initialized to 0 for each doParse() call and incremented via setLogIndex(logIndex), so that if two record files are processed in the same batch and share logIndex state (due to the AtomicInteger being passed by reference), a TOKENUNFREEZE item in the second file gets an incorrect logIndex that causes it to be deduplicated or overwritten by a TOKENFREEZE from the first file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between the synchronized keyword and Spring's @Transactional proxy, where the transaction is started by the proxy before the synchronized block is entered, allowing a concurrent thread to read the token_account freeze status between transaction start and the synchronized block, seeing a stale frozen state that is then cached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a record file where a TOKENFREEZE transaction appears after a TOKENUNFREEZE in the items list (both within the dateRangeFilter), and if the forEach processing order does not match the consensus timestamp order, the freeze is applied last, resulting in a permanently frozen account even though the on-chain order was unfreeze-then-freeze?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that parserContext.add(recordFile) is called after all items are processed, so that if the RecordFile object's state is mutated between item processing and the add() call (e.g., via aggregator.update(recordFile) setting loadEnd), the parserContext stores an inconsistent RecordFile that causes downstream flush operations to fail, rolling back the TOKENUNFREEZE?
